brand.aboutText:
I am an Architect with experience in building design and site management, architectural visualization and 3D, BIM modeling and coordination. Over time I evolved toward Fullstack Development, specializing in this area. I see a shared essence between constructing buildings and writing code: both require design, planning, and structured problem solving.

My transition into development started by building automation tools (Python, Dynamo) and BIM web apps (IFC.js, Three.js). Today I apply that same engineering mindset to build modern applications with the MERN stack, Next.js, and AI-assisted workflows.
brand.aboutStatement: I bring a multidisciplinary perspective that connects digital construction with software architecture, designing robust solutions from database to interface.

hero.subtitle: My approach combines an analytical profile with a strong digital mindset. I handle projects from spatial conception through modeling, management, and automation. I specialize in the AECO ecosystem and in building tools with tangible impact on decision-making and time optimization. I am not a programmer in the strict sense: I am a professional who uses programming to build solutions that integrate design, data, digitalization, and AI.

items.name: Control Manager
items.summary: Quality control plugin for Autodesk Revit
items.description: Desktop plugin for Autodesk Revit aimed at BIM coordinators who need to validate models consistently and with traceability. Control Manager automates common QC checks in Revit models and delivers actionable results. Review happens on the local model, with no dependency on external services.
items.context: BIM issue validation such as empty names, duplicate marks, empty comments, and unclosed rooms was done manually and took too much time.
items.impact: Speeds up BIM coordination reviews and improves delivery quality by catching issues before models are published.
items.role: Plugin architecture design, multi-version validation rules, direct selection of elements in Revit, and Excel export for tracking.
items.platform: Plugin for Autodesk Revit 2023–2027 on Windows 10–11 (64-bit)
items.scope: On-premise BIM in production; install via ZIP bundle or EXE installer

items.name: Ribbon Revit
items.description: PyRevit command ribbon that groups modeling, documentation, and QA scripts in one place. Workflows share project conventions and in-Revit feedback messages, aligning the team and reducing ad-hoc, hard-to-trace scripts.
items.context: An organized Revit tab to practice transactions, parameters, selection and filters, `FilteredElementCollector`, dialogs (WinForms and Task Dialog), and views, instead of scattered scripts that are hard to navigate.
items.impact: The **ARQFI** tab groups panels by theme (buttons, selection, parameters, transactions, collector, I/O, views, and graphic overrides) with sample buttons, a reusable `lib/Snippets` folder, and shortcuts to Learn Revit API and pyRevit/Revit API docs.
items.role: Organization of the `learnrevitapi.extension` following pyRevit conventions (`ARQFI.tab`, panels/stacks, `bundle.yaml`) and per-command scripts. Maintenance of `lib/Snippets` and `lib/Code` as decoupled practice material.
items.platform: Installable pyRevit extension for Autodesk Revit 2022, 2023, and 2024; Python via the IronPython or CPython runtime from your pyRevit install.
items.scope: Practice material for students and BIM developers.

items.name: LOL Match
items.summary: Web app for League of Legends players
items.description: Fullstack web app developed as a master’s thesis (TFM), letting League of Legends players manage profiles, form squads, find matches, and connect with the community. Includes authentication, profile management, real-time chat, a squad system, Riot Games API integration, and LoL statistics views.
items.context: There is no official place that combines LoL profiles, squads, matchmaking, and community chat. Players need a space to discover compatible profiles with good Honor and matching roles.
items.impact: Live platform with solid authentication (JWT), real-time messaging, Riot Games integration and statistics, with a responsive UI across devices.
items.role: React and Vite client (routes, WebSocket contexts, API services); on the server, Express REST API with MongoDB/Mongoose, Socket.io for chat and notifications, and cached Riot Games API calls.
items.platform: Responsive fullstack web app: React + Vite SPA with Node.js REST API.
items.scope: Master thesis with separate frontend and backend repos deployed on Vercel and Render.

items.name: Stockearly
items.description: MVP built for a small restaurant. Provides manual flows to log sales, purchases, and waste while consuming real-time updates from the backend via Socket.io. Its main goal is to grow into an inventory system that supports cost adjustments and ingredient optimization. Replaces scattered spreadsheets with a clear domain model. Includes a stable API and an operational browser-based dashboard. Data is stored in MongoDB.
items.context: Inventory split across spreadsheets and loosely aligned manual records. An event-driven core with coherent stock and a live operational view was needed without demanding full integrations from day one.
items.impact: Sales, purchases, and shrinkage go through an event bus that updates MongoDB and pushes changes to the dashboard instantly; hooks are ready for POS webhooks and an invoice pipeline to scale without redesigning the domain.
items.role: Modeled event-driven flows and stock services in Express, manual endpoints, and webhook. React (Vite) client with Material UI and Tailwind, Zustand state, and Socket.io for snapshots and live inventory.
items.platform: Frontend and backend: React + Vite SPA with event-driven Node/Express API, MongoDB, and WebSockets.
items.scope: Manual-first MVP with live dashboard and alerts. The same architecture can take POS webhooks, JSON invoices, and OCR-style extensions without breaking the model.

items.name: IFC Viewer
items.summary: 3D viewer with ThatOpen Engine and Three.js
items.description: IFC model web viewer built with That Open Engine (v3), Three.js, and Vite. The app follows a layered hexagonal architecture (domain, application, infrastructure, presentation) to isolate business rules from the concrete viewer implementation. Built with ThatOpen Engine and Three.js for remote coordination without desktop viewers. Supports IFC 2x3 and IFC4 models.
items.context: IFC viewer in the browser with property inspection and BIM navigation (levels and categories) without desktop installs or engine assets hosted from unreliable external origins.
items.impact: Loads IFC from file or sample model; classification by storeys and categories with isolate/hide/show; camera views and HUD view cube; web-ifc WASM served from the same site.
items.role: Application, infrastructure, and presentation layers; integration of `IfcLoader`, `FragmentsManager`, Highlighter, Classifier/Hider, and `ThatOpenViewerAdapter`, plus light/dark theme, camera presets, and visualization styles.
items.platform: TypeScript SPA with Vite, That Open Engine v3, Three.js, and web-ifc runtime. `@thatopen/fragments` worker bundled with the build.
items.scope: Viewer and model, selection and properties, and BIM navigation. Unit tests aligned with documented use cases.

items.name: Data Extractor
items.description: Web app to explore Autodesk Docs/ACC and OSS, translate RVT/NWC models with APS Model Derivative, and query properties/metadata with a UX focused on technical extraction. Use your own Autodesk account for cloud models or test with a demo account.
items.context: Ability to browse Autodesk Docs/ACC hubs, operate OSS buckets in the app, and queue RVT/NWC translations with Model Derivative without wiring scattered APS calls and manifests by hand.
items.impact: Unified Express backend with OAuth 3-legged access to hubs and a parallel OSS project, smart manifest and `svf2` jobs with `svf` fallback, property panel and aggregated metrics in React, and an optional demo mode with limits and CAPTCHA.
items.role: Express API implementation (`/auth`, Data Management, OSS, translate, metadata, properties, tree, demo) and React frontend with smart extraction. Docker added for dev and production-style deploys, with separate frontend/backend deployment documented.
items.platform: React client (dev 3001) and REST Express API (3000) with session cookies. APS Authentication, Data Management, OSS, and Model Derivative integration.
items.scope: Fullstack monorepo: authenticated Docs/ACC, app-owned OSS (buckets, upload, extraction), and optional public demo extraction without login.

items.name: MSc. Architecture, Design, and Architectural Engineering

items.description: Architect degree (6 years): structural design, construction systems, urban planning, and architectural engineering.

items.name: Master in BIM-Oriented Programming
items.description: ARCH, STR, MEP, and IFC modeling. Scripting with Dynamo and Python. Databases. Revit API.

items.name: Master BIM Software Developer
items.description: BIM web app development with IFC.js and Three.js. Open BIM standards and IFC schema.

items.name: PyRevit and Revit API Course
items.description: Custom tools and automations using the Revit API and the PyRevit framework.

items.name: Master in Fullstack Development
items.description: Web development, software engineering, AI integrations, and automation (n8n, MCP servers). Applied to BIM tool development: REST APIs, database modeling, and front-end interfaces for BIM data.

items.name: Autodesk Authorized Developer
items.meta: Autodesk Developer Network (ADN) member · ID: SEES0029
items.description: Develop integrations, automation, and custom solutions built on Autodesk’s official technologies and APIs.
